title: Min Stack
description:
Min Stack

Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.

Implement the MinStack class:

  • MinStack() initializes the stack object
  • void push(int val) pushes the element val onto the stack
  • void pop() removes the element on the top of the stack
  • int top() gets the top element of the stack
  • int getMin() retrieves the minimum element in the stack

You must implement a solution with O(1) time complexity for each function.

Example:

MinStack minStack = new MinStack();
minStack.push(-2);
minStack.push(0);
minStack.push(-3);
minStack.getMin(); // return -3
minStack.pop();
minStack.top();    // return 0
minStack.getMin(); // return -2

Constraints:

  • -231 <= val <= 231 - 1
  • Methods pop, top and getMin operations will always be called on non-empty stacks
  • At most 3 * 104 calls will be made to push, pop, top, and getMin
solution_text:
Min Stack

Algorithm (Auxiliary Min Stack):

  1. Maintain two stacks in parallel: the main stack and a min_stack
  2. On each push, compute the running minimum and push it onto min_stack
  3. On each pop, pop both stacks together to keep them in sync
  4. getMin() simply reads the top of min_stack

Time Complexity: O(1) for all operations

Space Complexity: O(n) — the auxiliary stack mirrors the main stack

Key Insights:

  • The minimum at any point depends on which elements are currently in the stack — it changes as elements are pushed and popped
  • By shadowing every push with the running minimum, the current minimum is always available in O(1) at the top of min_stack
  • Popping both stacks together ensures min_stack always reflects the minimum of the current main stack contents
